statement: Create an end-to-end product research tool with full traceability from requirements discovery through rule enforcement
description: The system shall support the complete research lifecycle: capturing sources, decomposing requirements, recording design resolutions, and deriving enforceable rules — all linked in a traceable knowledge graph so that any rule can be explained back to its originating requirement and source material.

source_refs.clause: Core architecture and data model definition
End-to-end traceability chain: SOURCE -> REQUIREMENT -> RESOLUTION -> RULE
Predecessor session-based design that evolved into recursive requirements

statement: Implement a normalized knowledge graph data model with 4 core artifact types (Source, Requirement, Resolution, Rule) and typed graph edges
description: The data model uses separate tables per artifact type with a junction table for 9 typed graph edges (references, refines_into, depends_on, contradicts, supersedes, needs, resolves, spawns, produces). Requirements recurse to arbitrary depth via parentId. Each artifact carries its own status lifecycle.

source_refs.clause: Data Model: 4 Types -- Source, Requirement, Resolution, Rule
Rule types, severity, modality, coverage levels

statement: Build a real-time collaborative Convex backend with full CRUD, graph queries, and traceability functions
description: Convex backend at provenance/convex/ with 10 tables, 27 indexes, entity modules for all artifact types, graph.ts with 9 query functions (getCanvasScope, getDepthScope, getRootOverview, traceForward, traceBackward, findGaps, findStale, findOrphans, getCoverageStats), HTTP API endpoints, and WorkOS JWT authentication.

source_refs.clause: 6-phase Convex backend implementation plan

statement: Implement full Statesman CLI integration for all provenance artifact operations
description: CLI commands under 'statesman provenance' covering requirements, sources, resolutions, rules, edges, threads, messages, graph queries, and traceability queries. ProvenanceClient handles Convex Function and HTTP API calls with retry, null-field stripping, and automatic users:store preflight.

source_refs.clause: F2: Project Discovery, F3: Project Creation, F4: Provenance Core CRUD

statement: Build a React Flow spatial canvas UI with progressive disclosure and recursive navigation
description: Web UI at statesman-web with React Flow canvas, ELK auto-layout with zone partitioning, 7 node types (source, requirement, resolution, rule, topic, question, boundary), 13 edge types with visual hierarchy, semantic zoom with drill-down, Focus+Context depth view, creation sidebar, and breadcrumb navigation.

source_refs.clause: 8-phase canvas enhancement with ELK layout and semantic zoom
Mock-to-live backend conversion for all canvas components

statement: Support forward and backward traceability queries across the full artifact chain
description: Forward traceability: Source -> Requirements -> Resolutions -> Rules (what's affected when a source changes). Backward traceability: Rule -> Resolution -> Requirement -> Source (why does this rule exist). Both exposed via CLI commands and HTTP API endpoints.

source_refs.clause: Traceability: forward (source changed, what's affected) and backward (why does this rule exist)

statement: Implement one-directional rule sync from Provenance to the Statesman broker
description: Broker sync module (broker.ts, 892 lines) syncs rules from Provenance to the broker via POST /rules. Includes batch sync, coverage refresh, hourly cron job, camelCase-to-snake_case mapping, and broker URL resolution via control plane or environment variable.

source_refs.clause: Phase 6: Broker Sync and Traceability

statement: Provide a durable thread and message discussion system attachable to any artifact
description: Threads attach to any artifact type (requirement, source, resolution, rule, topic, question) with status lifecycle (active, resolved, archived). Messages carry role (user, assistant, system) for attributing human vs AI-authored content. Convenience CLI command auto-creates threads.

statement: Implement WorkOS SSO authentication with project-scoped authorization
description: WorkOS integration for both Convex (first-party @convex-dev/workos) and control plane. JWT validation, user record upsert, project access checks. All writes attributable to human identity (seat owner). API key exchange for non-interactive CLI usage.

source_refs.clause: F1: Authentication Persistence, F7: Authorization Runtime Compatibility

statement: Support external test coverage linking via source-code annotations and static analysis, enabling rule traceability without requiring adoption of the Statesman contract framework
description: Not all tests fit into Statesman's contract scope (perf tests, unit tests, existing suites). Teams need a low-friction way to annotate existing tests in any language to declare which Provenance rules they enforce. A static analysis scanner extracts these annotations and reports coverage to Provenance, creating traceability links from rules to external test evidence.

statement: Define an annotation format that mirrors Statesman's existing contract metadata vocabulary for declaring rule coverage in external test code
description: Adopt Allure's label/link primitive model but use Statesman's own vocabulary. The annotation fields replicate what contracts already carry: name, description, tags (from Contract trait), rule code, coverage level, confidence, notes (from RuleContract join). Per-language native APIs (Python decorators, Rust attributes, Java annotations, JS/TS functions) all serialize to the same output format. Comment-based annotations as the zero-dependency universal baseline. Explicitly NO epic/feature/story -- Statesman is not a project management tool. The requirement tree provides hierarchy; Provenance derives structural context from the rule's graph chain.

source_refs.clause: Complete annotation format specification

statement: Build a static analysis scanner in the Statesman CLI that extracts rule annotations from source code and reports coverage to Provenance, giving external tests access to the full traceability, impact analysis, gap detection, and audit trail capabilities
description: The scanner traverses a codebase, finds test files, extracts @statesman annotations (mirroring contract metadata: name, description, tags, rule, coverage, confidence), and pushes structured coverage to the broker via RuleContract with mapping_source=imported. By living in the CLI, external tests become first-class citizens in the Provenance graph: traceability queries show them alongside contracts, traceForward surfaces affected tests when sources change, findGaps detects rules with no coverage from any source, the UI renders them in the same canvas, all writes carry audit trails, and CI pipelines get coverage reporting with one additional step. Output JSON for machine consumption. Handle comment syntax variations across languages. Includes scan-time validation: cross-reference extracted annotations against active project rules and warn on unknown rule codes, deprecated rules, archived rules to prevent false coverage confidence.

statement: Provide first-class 'statesman coverage' commands at the top level spanning both contract and external test coverage
description: Top-level 'statesman coverage scan --path ./tests' scans for annotations and pushes to Provenance. 'statesman coverage report' accepts pre-computed scan results from stdin for CI flexibility. Supports path filters, file type filters, dry-run mode, JSON/markdown output. On definitive changes going into source (not dry-runs), post diffs to rule threads in Provenance so the audit trail captures what changed: new test coverage, removed coverage, changed confidence/coverage levels. First-class placement under 'statesman coverage' rather than nested under 'provenance' because coverage spans both contract coverage from the broker and external test evidence from annotations.

statement: Store external test coverage in the broker alongside contract coverage, using the existing RuleContract model with mapping_source=imported
description: External test evidence is pushed to the broker via /rules/{code}/coverage with mapping_source=imported. The broker is the single source of truth for all coverage data regardless of origin. Provenance pulls from the broker via fetchRuleCoverage unchanged -- no new Convex tables, no merge logic. The broker's /rules/gaps and /rules/coverage endpoints get richer immediately. Future broker capabilities (risk scoring, drift detection, transaction correlation with declared coverage) benefit from having external test data co-located with contract coverage.

statement: Detect and report stale annotations where rule codes reference rules that no longer exist or have been deprecated
description: As rules evolve (deprecated, archived, superseded), annotations in test code can go stale. The scanner should cross-reference extracted annotations against the project's active rules and flag mismatches: unknown rule codes, deprecated rules, archived rules. This prevents false coverage confidence.

statement: Support future runtime test reporters that capture pass/fail results for annotated tests
description: Static analysis establishes the annotation convention. Runtime reporters (Jest plugin, pytest plugin, JUnit extension, Go test reporter) will reuse the same annotation format but additionally capture test execution results and report pass/fail per rule. This requirement captures the extension point -- the annotation format and Provenance API must be designed to accept runtime evidence when it becomes available, without breaking the static-analysis-only path.

statement: Expose parent-id filtering on 'statesman provenance requirements list' to allow listing children of a specific requirement without client-side filtering
description: The Convex backend has getChildren but the CLI only exposes list with project-id scoping. Agents and humans working with requirement trees need to list children directly. Add --parent-id flag to requirements list that delegates to the getChildren query.

statement: The system must support role-based access control (RBAC) with fine-grained per-artifact-type permissions for both human users and AI agents, using WorkOS as the role/permission authority for humans and a control-plane agent identity layer for machines.
description: Covers: WorkOS RBAC activation (roles, permissions in JWT), agent identity model (agents table, agent_tokens, sm_agent_ prefix), permission enforcement in Provenance mutations, and attribution of agent actions via synthetic user records.

statement: The system must support role-based access control (RBAC) with fine-grained per-artifact-type permissions for both human users and AI agents, using WorkOS as the role/permission authority for humans and a control-plane agent identity layer for machines.
description: Covers: WorkOS RBAC activation (roles, permissions in JWT), agent identity model (agents table, agent_tokens, sm_agent_ prefix), permission enforcement in Provenance mutations, and attribution of agent actions via synthetic user records.

source_refs.clause: Full document: agent auth model, WorkOS RBAC integration, revised architecture

statement: The Provenance CLI shall support direct lookup of artifacts by their domain identifier (rule_code for rules, slug for sources, etc.) without requiring pagination through list results
description: Currently the only way to find a rule by its code (e.g. NS-015) is to paginate through the full list sorted newest-first. With 73 rules at 50 per page, this is unusable for both humans and agents. Need: statesman provenance rules get --rule-code NS-015. Same pattern for other artifact types with domain identifiers.

statement: The Provenance CLI list commands shall support filtering by field values and configurable sort order
description: Currently list commands return all artifacts sorted newest-first with no filtering. At scale (73+ rules), this makes targeted queries impossible without client-side processing. Need: --filter 'severity=critical', --filter 'ruleCode=NS-01*', --sort=oldest, --sort=rule-code. Applies to rules list, requirements list, resolutions list, sources list.

statement: The Provenance CLI shall support bulk import and export of artifacts in JSON or TOML format
description: Creating 59 rules required 59 separate CLI calls. A bulk operation (statesman provenance rules import rules.json) would reduce this to one call. Export enables backup, migration, and offline review. Format should match the JSON output of list commands for round-trip compatibility.

statement: The Provenance thread post command shall reliably create threads and post messages on any artifact type without intermittent failures
description: During noscope planning, 3 of 6 agent sessions failed to post thread comments on rules. Errors included 'artifact not found' for valid artifact IDs. Earlier agents in the same session succeeded with identical commands. Root cause unclear: possibly auth token expiry mid-session, Convex HTTP endpoint routing, or rate limiting. Thread posting is critical for agent-driven review workflows.

statement: asdf

The Provenance CLI shall serve agent-navigable HAL+JSON responses by default, with _links indicating available operations and related artifacts, so that agents can discover and follow graph relationships without prior knowledge of CLI commands
description: Currently --json outputs raw Convex documents with opaque internal IDs and no navigability hints. An agent receiving a rule listing sees jx71svs9vrs... but has no indication that statesman provenance traceability or statesman provenance graph exist as follow-up commands. The broker already serves HAL+JSON with _links (broker/src/api/hal.rs). The CLI provenance commands should follow the same pattern so agents can navigate the graph by following links rather than guessing commands. Research backing: Graph Navigability brainstorming (2026-03-29) identified agents fail to use Provenance because they cannot discover connections from raw output. Engagement Context contract (noscope project) specifies agents should receive self-contained bundles. Agent UX brainstorming (2026-03-20) established CLI as the chokepoint for agent interaction.

statement: The broker must enforce multi-tenant security across all API endpoints
description: Covers authentication pipeline (Bearer-only, JWT RS256, API key delegation), authorization model (hierarchical permissions, RequirePermission extractors), tenant isolation (project-scoped SQL, ownership verification, cross-org prevention), input validation (progressive validation, pagination caps, config validation), information leakage prevention (404-not-403, token prefix logging, generic errors), infrastructure security (timeouts, fail-closed, parameterized SQL), and audit trail (project_id tracing, identity attribution, content-addressed dedup) for all broker HTTP handlers, services, and storage repositories.

source_refs.clause: broker/src/auth/, broker/src/api/handlers/, broker/src/services/, broker/src/storage/

statement: Provenance shall support structured multi-persona ideation threads for artifact review and requirement discovery
description: The system shall let automated jobs convene 3-5 distinct persona-style agents around a target requirement, source, resolution, rule, or problem. The agents leave attributable thread comments, challenge one another, and produce a synthesis that helps humans discover requirements, risks, rules, resolutions, and open questions while preserving dissent and traceability.

source_refs.clause: User-validated pattern: named agents ideating in Provenance threads produce useful design outcomes and should be productionised as bounded automated review jobs

statement: Ideation runs shall be bounded artifact-attached thread discussions with participant comments and synthesis
description: Each ideation run targets one Provenance artifact or problem, creates or uses an artifact thread, runs a bounded set of persona participants, records their individual comments, and posts a synthesis that future reviewers can inspect without losing the original reasoning.

source_refs.clause: Ideation should happen in artifact threads with individual participant comments and a synthesis

statement: Persona profiles shall be versioned prompt identities with explicit attribution and execution metadata
description: A persona used in an ideation run is a versioned prompt/profile with a name, purpose, prompt version, model/config metadata, executor identity, and owner attribution. The profile may be evocative, but the audit trail must distinguish persona from the authenticated agent or human owner.

source_refs.clause: Agents inherit named personas/souls for ideation; production system must preserve attribution and profile versioning

statement: Ideation synthesis shall preserve dissent, evidence quality, and source grounding
description: The synthesis of a multi-persona ideation run must distinguish consensus, contested claims, unsupported speculation, source-backed evidence, open questions, and assumptions. It must not launder disagreement into false confidence.

source_refs.clause: Prior named-agent debates were useful because they surfaced dissent, critique, and evidence gaps

statement: Ideation job writes shall be idempotent, retry-safe, and visibly partial on failure
description: Automated ideation may involve multiple model calls and thread posts. Re-running or retrying a job must not duplicate comments, synthesis, proposals, or artifact links. If some participants fail or thread posting fails, the run must expose partial status and diagnostic context.

source_refs.clause: Automated multi-agent jobs depend on reliable, retry-safe thread posting

statement: Ideation outputs shall require human promotion before becoming canonical requirements, resolutions, rules, or work items
description: Persona comments and synthesis may propose artifacts, but generated proposals remain suggestions until a human explicitly accepts, edits, or rejects them. Promotion actions must link the new artifact back to the source thread messages and run metadata.

source_refs.clause: Debate outputs are useful for arriving at solutions but should feed human-reviewed Provenance artifacts

statement: Ideation runs shall have a first-class lifecycle, correlation identity, and inspectable execution state
description: A multi-persona ideation run is not just a cluster of thread messages. The run must have a stable identity, target artifact, input snapshot, participant set, status lifecycle, retry/attempt state, and machine-readable inspection surface so agents and humans can validate what happened.

source_refs.clause: Productionising persona ideation requires stable run identity, lifecycle state, and inspectable execution state

statement: Ideation synthesis shall emit typed proposal cards with traceable promotion states
description: Synthesis must produce structured candidate changes that can be accepted, rejected, deferred, or superseded. Proposal cards must be typed by target artifact kind and linked to supporting participant comments, evidence, dissent, confidence, and human decisions.

source_refs.clause: Ideation should produce proposed artifact changes that humans can accept, reject, or defer

statement: Ideation implementation shall provide validation hooks and tests proving product-shape conformance
description: An implementation agent must be able to validate that a built ideation run fits the intended product shape: one target, one active thread, bounded participant outputs, synthesis, proposal cards, preserved dissent, human-gated promotion, idempotent retries, and inspectable failure state.

source_refs.clause: Good rule coverage should let implementation agents validate the feature fits the intended product shape

statement: Persona participant comments shall follow a structured contribution contract
description: Each persona participant comment should be useful as review evidence, not just prose. Participant outputs must identify stance, findings, evidence, risks, objections, suggested actions, uncertainty, and references to prior participant claims when challenging another persona.

source_refs.clause: Persona comments are useful only when they are structured enough to preserve findings, objections, and evidence

statement: Ideation thread presentation shall be outcome-first while preserving participant traceability
description: The user-facing thread surface should prioritize synthesis, proposals, dissent, and failures before raw transcript. Participant comments and raw model output remain available for audit, but the default reading experience must not become transcript sludge.

source_refs.clause: Thread-based ideation should remain readable and outcome-first while preserving participant traceability

statement: Ideation jobs shall enforce scope, budget, safety, and retention limits
description: Automated multi-persona review must be bounded by project scope, target artifact scope, source access policy, participant count, rounds, token/time budget, cancellation, rate limits, retention policy, and unsafe-output handling.

source_refs.clause: Automated persona ideation jobs must be bounded and governed before production use
